channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ukraine Launches Drone Strikes on Moscow Amid Domestic Political Tensions
item.description: Ukraine has launched a significant drone attack on Moscow, with over 400 drones targeting the Russian capital, according to the city's mayor. This comes amid escalating tensions between Ukraine and Ru...

item.title: Cultural stigma and environmental challenges facing birdwatchers in Nigeria
item.description: The article explores the challenges faced by birdwatchers in Nigeria, highlighting the cultural stigma and misconceptions surrounding the activity. It discusses how traditional beliefs often view bird...

item.title: Nigeria-Morocco Gas Pipeline Aims to Boost African Energy Integration
item.description: The Nigeria-Morocco gas pipeline (NMGP), a €23bn project spanning 6,000km across 13 African countries, aims to connect West African gas resources to regional markets and Europe. Signed by Ecowas leade...

item.title: Protests Against Education Minister's Irregularities in India
item.description: Indian police used tear gas and baton charges to disperse protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) who were attempting to march towards parliament to demand the resignation of Education Ministe...

item.title: Man Resists Arrest, Injures Officers in Car Park Incident
item.description: A 30-year-old man, Rhys Haralambos Gregoriou, allegedly injured two police officers during a struggle in a car park in Narrabundah after resisting arrest. The incident occurred around 2:50am on Monday...

item.title: Effective Mosquito Control Methods for Yard Management
item.description: The article discusses a personal experience in Washington, D.C., where the author successfully reduced the mosquito population in their yard using a method inspired by strategies from the Philippines,...

item.title: Rea Vaya bus services suspended again, leaving commuters stranded
item.description: Rea Vaya’s Phase 1B bus services have been suspended since Friday, leaving thousands of commuters stranded and forcing them to find alternative transport. The bus service informed passengers via socia...

item.title: Understanding Ultimate and Evidentiary Facts in Duterte's Impeachment Case
item.description: The Senate impeachment court continued the trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, focusing on her alleged unexplained wealth. House prosecutor Chel Diokno outlined 'ultimate facts'—the core elements of...

item.title: Saboti MP Caleb Amisi Launches School Bus Programme with Donation to Masinde Muliro Senior School
item.description: Saboti Member of Parliament Caleb Amisi has officially handed over a brand new school bus to Masinde Muliro Senior School in Tuwan Ward, Trans Nzoia County, as part of his wider plan to strengthen edu...

item.title: Kenyan Comedian Reacts to Spain's World Cup Win and Calls from Fans
item.description: Popular Kenyan comedian Njugush shared a video reacting to Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final. The comedian, who had been wearing an Argentina jersey during...

item.title: Gayton McKenzie's R31m World Cup Spending Breakdown
item.description: Sports Minister Gayton McKenzie detailed the R31m World Cup delegation budget, clarifying that only 75 individuals were directly funded by the department. The delegation included 18 officials, 30 arti...

item.title: Simba SC eyes Cameroon Premier League top scorer Kooh for potential transfer
item.description: Simba SC, a Tanzanian football club, has shown interest in signing Cameroon Premier League top scorer Kooh. The move comes as part of the club's strategy to strengthen its squad for upcoming matches. ...

item.title: Philippine Navy Officer Injured in China Coast Guard Confrontation at Ayungin
item.description: A Philippine Navy personnel was injured after members of the China Coast Guard (CCG) allegedly assaulted him during a confrontation in Ayungin (Second Thomas) Shoal on July 20, 2026, according to the ...

item.title: Protester Challenges Security Forces at Jantar Mantar During CJP March to Parliament
item.description: A protest organized by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) marched towards Parliament from Jantar Mantar, facing police intervention with lathi charges and tear gas. A viral video shows a man confronting ...

item.title: Trump Administration Withheld Details of Iranian Attacks on U.S. Forces
item.description: The Trump administration has been accused of withholding critical information about Iranian attacks on U.S. military forces, according to a report by The New York Times. While the Pentagon publicly di...

item.title: Ryanair Reports 34% Drop in Profits Amid Rising Fuel Costs and Fare Cuts
item.description: Ryanair, the budget airline group, has reported a significant decline in its after-tax profits for the three months ending June, with a 34 per cent drop to €538 million. This downturn is primarily att...

item.title: Trump's Vision for Hosting the 2026 World Cup Demonstrates American Excellence
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, executive director of the White House Task Force on the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlights President Trump’s pivotal role in organizing the event. The tournament, held across the U.S., M...

item.title: Canoe Capsizes in Jigawa: Six Dead, 15 Rescued
item.description: A canoe accident in Jigawa State's Ringim Local Government Area claimed six lives and rescued 15 people, with several others still missing. The incident occurred on July 20, 2026, around 4 pm in Malam...

item.title: Tanzania promotes natural heritage through UNESCO meeting
item.description: Tanzania has leveraged the 48th UNESCO General Assembly to showcase its diverse natural heritage, emphasizing the importance of preserving ecosystems and cultural landscapes. The country's participati...

item.title: Soldiers' salary review sparks debate on adequacy amid rising costs in Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian government has doubled the minimum monthly salary for soldiers from N49,000 to N100,000, but civil society organizations (CSOs) and advocacy groups argue that this increase does not match...
